# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:22.04

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    nginx \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create non-root user
RUN useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash appuser
USER appuser
WORKDIR /home/appuser

# Copy application files
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip3 install --user -r requirements.txt

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Health checks
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:80/health || exit 1

# Start all services
CMD ["./start_services.sh"]
